pattern_1_governance_livelihood_nexus.description: Exclusion from decision-making and limited finance appear together as interconnected barriers
pattern_1_governance_livelihood_nexus.interpretation: Applicants understand coastal resilience requires both voice in governance AND economic security

pattern_2_climate_habitat_finance_trilog.description: Among applicants with 4+ barriers, climate impacts, habitat loss, and limited finance form core combination

pattern_3_regional_collaboration_as_norm.description: Majority of applicants mention regional collaboration keywords
pattern_3_regional_collaboration_as_norm.interpretation: High demand for cross-border peer learning and ASEAN-level collaboration

pattern_4_policy_engagement_gradient.description: Tiered capacity from awareness to integration
pattern_4_policy_engagement_gradient.interpretation: Gradient suggests tiered capacity: awareness → engagement → integration

pattern_5_gender_and_youth_leadership.description: Strong representation of traditionally marginalized voices
pattern_5_gender_and_youth_leadership.interpretation: Program attracts emerging leaders; opportunity for mentorship pathways

strategic_insights.finding: 68.1% regional interest indicates ASEAN-level programming would be highly valued
While 64.9% show governance awareness, only 26.6% demonstrate deep policy integration, suggesting capacity-building need
Exclusion from decision-making (68.1%) and limited finance (43.6%) dominate over resource depletion issues, indicating human-centered approach
44.7% of applicants from Indonesia suggests strong institutional capacity in one country; opportunity for regional knowledge transfer
19.1% from women-led/youth-led groups indicates program attracts emerging leaders; opportunity for mentorship pathways
68.1% mention 2+ barriers indicates sophisticated understanding of interconnected coastal challenges

applicants.name: Osaias Soares
applicants.organization: Association of Tourism Koleku Mahanak Atauro
